# FM cockpit AgentCard component — the resident card at the artifact bar

## Context

T2.7 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — the cockpit's atom: the per-agent card composing the #14593 primitives into the SSOT artifact's card anatomy (family rail · state dot+pulse · name · engine tag · state line · current-lane line · foot meta + per-card controls slot). Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The card is where the product principles and the ADR 0032 render rules meet the pixel: *state reads at a glance* (form-encoded status), *real-time is the spine* (live lane-line), and the anti-lock-in contract at card grain. Hand-rolling it per view (grid, detail header, pop-out title) would fork the resident's visual identity across surfaces.

## The Architectural Reality

- Composes #14593 primitives (StateDot/FamilyRail/HealthSwatch) + #14578 tokens; namespace per the #14577 gate.
- **ADR 0032 bindings at card grain (the first consumer):** name renders as mutable display state over the durable id (§2.3.2); the engine tag ("opus-4.8" / "fable-5" in the mock) is **session metadata, never identity** (§2.3.7) — bound from the current `EmbodiedEpisode`-era data when #11318 lands, from the roster DTO until then; the family rail re-renders in place on family change, same resident (§2.3.3 fixture).
- Wire: consumes the Lane-2 roster/status DTO (#14571, approved per its owner) + #14595 runtime-status — the card binds LIVE per map v1.1 (no mocks; degrade-honestly when the wire is down: stale renders as stale, the #13444 freshness discipline at card grain).
- The per-view triplet: component + unit specs + NL-verifiable render.

## The Fix

One PR: (1) AgentCard (config-driven: identity id, display name + provenance slot, engine tag, state, lane line, foot meta, controls slot — controls themselves are T5); (2) unit specs — state→primitive mapping, name-as-display-state binding (rename re-renders, same card instance), engine-tag-as-metadata, stale-wire render; (3) NL-verifiable mount (dev-server card wall bound to the live roster).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Card renders the SSOT anatomy from #14593/#14578 exclusively (zero local colors/spacing).
- [ ] Name binding: a display-name change re-renders in place — same instance, no re-key (ADR 0032 §2.3.2 fixture).
- [ ] Engine tag binds session/era metadata; a tag change never re-keys the card (§2.3.7 fixture).
- [ ] Family-rail rebind re-renders in place (§2.3.3 fixture).
- [ ] Live wire binding (#14571 DTO + #14595 status) with stale-as-stale degrade; no mock path in the shipped code.
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable render wall.

## Out of Scope

Controls behavior (T5) · grid layout (T2.8) · detail view (T4) · primitives/tokens (#14593/#14578) · wire methods (Lane 2: #14571/#14595).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by #14593 · consumes #14571/#14595 (Lane-2 seams, Ada) · ADR 0032 §2.3 (PR #14583) · #14592 density evidence (card-churn data) · D#14561.

Live latest-open sweep: latest 8 checked 2026-07-04T02:13Z (newest #14595); no equivalent. A2A sweep: clean (Ada's L2 claims are the consumed seams, boundary acked both directions). Structure-map gate: N/A — app-layer component.

- 2026-07-04T12:38:30Z @tobiu referenced in commit `5c09efc` - "refactor(agentos): StateDot → class-based component (functional→idiomatic) (#14745) (#14746)

Operator vetoed functional FM components ("vastly inferior to class-based, VBA"); VBA confirmed apps/ is 320:9 class-vs-functional. Converts StateDot from defineComponent to `extends Neo.component.Base` — reactive state_/live_ configs with afterSet render (--fm-dot via style, fm-live via cls), the src/component/Chip.mjs idiom. Public surface preserved: exported stateToken (hardened) + the --fm-* binding contract unchanged.

Pattern-setter for the FM-layer conversion (#14745): FamilyRail/EventChip/HealthSwatch follow, then AgentCard #14598 builds class-based. statePrimitives.spec.mjs passes 3/3 UNCHANGED — the class version is behavior-identical (Neo.create + initVnode + vdom.style/cls all hold)."
- 2026-07-04T12:43:29Z @tobiu referenced in commit `31698dd` - "refactor(agentos): FamilyRail/EventChip/HealthSwatch → class-based (#14745) (#14747)

Completes the FM primitive layer functional→class conversion (StateDot was PR #14746). Each now extends Neo.component.Base with reactive configs + afterSet render (the src/component/Chip.mjs idiom): FamilyRail (family_ → --fm-rail + unclassified cls), EventChip (kind_/label_ → --fm-chip + text), HealthSwatch (state_/count_/label_ → dot/count/label cn nodes, count removeDom-toggled). Exported resolvers (familyToken/isKnownFamily/stateLabel) + the --fm-* binding contract preserved.

Specs green: familyRail 4/4 + eventChip 3/3 + healthSwatch 6/6 = 13/13. One healthSwatch assertion updated to the class idiom (the count node is removeDom-toggled, not omitted from cn). AgentCard #14598 now builds class-based composing these."

- 2026-07-11T11:20:08Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by PR #15029
- 2026-07-11T11:39:03Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `d25b525` - "feat(agentos): AgentCard open-lane count badge — the density-evidence lane consequence (#14598)

The #14592 density evidence measured 7-17 open lanes per active agent: one
truncated lane line cannot carry that truth, so the lane row becomes
current-lane text + an open-lane count badge. The FleetAgent model gains a
nullable openLaneCount (Integer, defaultValue null); the card renders the
badge ONLY on a reported positive count — null/absent means the lane
producer has not spoken and renders NO badge (unknown never poses as zero;
zero itself is state-axis truth, not badge value). The dock-blueprint
factory maps the field through null-safe, the roster reconcile deliberately
OMITS it (producer ownership — a roster merge must never wipe what the lane
capability wrote), and the sample seed carries the MEASURED per-agent
counts from the evidence. Badge styling rides the token layer (var()-only)."
- 2026-07-11T12:55:29Z @neo-opus-vega cross-referenced by #15036
- 2026-07-11T12:55:53Z @neo-opus-vega referenced in commit `7dcc3df` - "feat(agentos): openLaneCount rides the roster DTO end-to-end — the badge survives the first authoritative load (#14598)

Review convergence (Euclid's first-live-load probe): the badge field was
seed-only — loadRoster's first authoritative snapshot clears the sample
Store and mapRosterRow omitted the field, so the wired cockpit erased the
badge. The fix makes the roster DTO the field's OWNING producer, the exact
launchable tri-state precedent: createFleetCockpitStatus hoists
openLaneCount from the assembler rows (null = not stamped, this pure map
derives nothing), mapRosterRow carries it onto the record, and the OMIT
rule now covers laneLine only. Regressions land on both hemispheres: the
assembler passthrough spec, and the requested first-authoritative-load
witness — a stamped live count is stored while an un-stamped row degrades
to an explicit null, so the seeded sample number can never pose as live
truth. CARD-CONTRACT.md gains the badge row (producer + degrade rule) and
the FleetAgent summary enumerates the roster-DTO tri-state truths. The
stamping producer (Brain-side enricher) is #15036."
- 2026-07-11T14:35:54Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6a05bdd` - "feat(agentos): AgentCard open-lane count badge — the density-evidence lane consequence (#14598) (#15029)

* feat(agentos): AgentCard open-lane count badge — the density-evidence lane consequence (#14598)

The #14592 density evidence measured 7-17 open lanes per active agent: one
truncated lane line cannot carry that truth, so the lane row becomes
current-lane text + an open-lane count badge. The FleetAgent model gains a
nullable openLaneCount (Integer, defaultValue null); the card renders the
badge ONLY on a reported positive count — null/absent means the lane
producer has not spoken and renders NO badge (unknown never poses as zero;
zero itself is state-axis truth, not badge value). The dock-blueprint
factory maps the field through null-safe, the roster reconcile deliberately
OMITS it (producer ownership — a roster merge must never wipe what the lane
capability wrote), and the sample seed carries the MEASURED per-agent
counts from the evidence. Badge styling rides the token layer (var()-only).

* feat(agentos): openLaneCount rides the roster DTO end-to-end — the badge survives the first authoritative load (#14598)

Review convergence (Euclid's first-live-load probe): the badge field was
seed-only — loadRoster's first authoritative snapshot clears the sample
Store and mapRosterRow omitted the field, so the wired cockpit erased the
badge. The fix makes the roster DTO the field's OWNING producer, the exact
launchable tri-state precedent: createFleetCockpitStatus hoists
openLaneCount from the assembler rows (null = not stamped, this pure map
derives nothing), mapRosterRow carries it onto the record, and the OMIT
rule now covers laneLine only. Regressions land on both hemispheres: the
assembler passthrough spec, and the requested first-authoritative-load
witness — a stamped live count is stored while an un-stamped row degrades
to an explicit null, so the seeded sample number can never pose as live
truth. CARD-CONTRACT.md gains the badge row (producer + degrade rule) and
the FleetAgent summary enumerates the roster-DTO tri-state truths. The
stamping producer (Brain-side enricher) is #15036."
